repo_prior_art.cite: none; Q1_MinimalBasis/Q1_StaggeredMinimalBasis cover binary switch-on contamination bases, not continuous-dose dCDH-vs-TWFE ACR rows

published_prior_art.cite: CallawayGoodmanBaconSantAnna2024 gives continuous-treatment decompositions and TWFE interpretation problems; deChaisemartinDHaultfoeuille2023SeveralTreatments gives a closer TWFE contamination formula for several treatments

repo_prior_art.cite: eid_bjs_efficient_event_study_v1 has a rank-zero IF frontier for binary event-study estimators, but not DHS/dCDH/TWFE continuous-dose ACR

published_prior_art.cite: DHaultfoeuilleHoderleinSasaki2023, deChaisemartinDHaultfoeuillePasquierSowVazquezBare2025, and CallawayGoodmanBaconSantAnna2024 identify/decompose separate continuous-treatment objects but do not state a three-method iff spectral equality frontier

repo_prior_art.cite: panel_sdid_mc_id_v1 asks for strict estimator-frontier witnesses in SCM/SDID/MC, not continuous-treatment DiD ACR functionals

published_prior_art.cite: deChaisemartinDHaultfoeuille2020, GoodmanBacon2021, and SunAbraham2021 prove TWFE contamination phenomena, but not open-set separation among DHS, dCDH, and continuous-dose TWFE ACR functionals

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The §8(b) claim that deChaisemartinDHaultfoeuilleVazquezBare2024 recommends an empirical TWFE diagnostic overstates the paper; the verified abstract warns TWFE is nonrobust and proposes continuous-treatment DiD without stayers, but not this residual-row diagnostic.

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1 | Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The proposal misses deChaisemartinDHaultfoeuille2023SeveralTreatments, a closer TWFE contamination result showing coefficients with several treatments are weighted sums of own effects plus contamination from other treatments.

soundness_flags.label: Locked parameters | Formal setup
soundness_flags.one_line: The target ACR(d)=d/dd E[Y_it(d)-Y_it(0)] leaves the period t and any cohort-period aggregation weights unspecified while the methods are defined over finite panels with cohorts and periods.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The claim that the three efficient influence scores have the same asymptotic variance is not well-posed until a common semiparametric model/tangent space and efficiency bound for DHS, dCDH, and TWFE are defined.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The DHS efficiency bound, dCDH excess-variance term, and TWFE bias proportionality are stated across different submodels without defining the nesting/common score space or the bandwidth-misspecification component.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Pin the ACR target as a specific period, adjacent-period contrast, or explicit cohort-period weighted population curve, then restate all three functionals against that same target.
Split the rank-zero equality result from the efficiency/variance result: first prove functional equality, then add a separate common-submodel variance corollary with a named tangent space and bound.
Add deChaisemartinDHaultfoeuille2023SeveralTreatments to §5 and use it to sharpen the TWFE contamination row rather than relying only on binary/event-study TWFE analogies.
Replace the vague operator pairing in Theorem 1 with a fully dimensioned residual-row formula, including the Hilbert/Frobenius inner product and the observable arguments of r_{dCDH,TWFE}(d).

recommended_next_step: Revise the target definition and common efficiency model first, then patch the literature comparison around the several-treatments TWFE paper and the AEA P&P diagnostic overclaim.
